lesson.title: Optimization
lesson.body: Applied optimization uses calculus to find the maximum or minimum of a real-world quantity. The strategy: identify the quantity to optimize (objective function), express it in terms of one variable using any constraint, then find critical points. Always verify whether a critical point is a maximum or minimum, and check the domain for endpoint considerations.

rooms.question: You have 100 m of fencing for a rectangular enclosure against a wall (no fencing needed on the wall side). The width is w and length along the wall is ℓ. The objective function for area is:
rooms.explanation: With constraint 2w + ℓ = 100, substitute ℓ = 100−2w into A = wℓ to get A(w) = 100w − 2w².

rooms.question: Continuing from above, the width w that maximizes area is:
A box with square base of side x and no top has surface area 300 cm². Volume is V = x²h. Express V as a function of x alone.
rooms.explanation: From surface area constraint h = (300−x²)/(4x). Substitute: V = x²·h = x(300−x²)/4.

rooms.question: Order the general optimization strategy:
items: Write it as a function of one variable using constraints
Find critical points (set derivative to zero)
Use 2nd derivative test or endpoints to confirm max/min
rooms.explanation: Optimization: define objective, reduce to one variable, differentiate and find critical points, verify the type of extremum.

rooms.question: Among all rectangles with perimeter P, which has maximum area?
rooms.explanation: Given P = 2(x+y), maximize A = xy subject to x+y = P/2. By AM-GM, A = x(P/2−x) is maximized when x = y = P/4 — a square.

boss.name: The Optimizer
boss.taunt: There is always a maximum. There is always a minimum. Find them.

questions.question: The minimum distance from the point (3, 0) to the parabola y = x² occurs at x =
questions.explanation: Minimize D² = (x−3)² + x⁴. Set d(D²)/dx = 2(x−3) + 4x³ = 0. Numerically x ≈ 1 is the solution.

questions.question: A manufacturer's revenue R(q) = 50q − q² and cost C(q) = 100 + 10q. Profit-maximizing quantity is:
questions.explanation: π = R − C = −q² + 40q − 100. π'(q) = −2q + 40 = 0 → q = 20. π''= −2 < 0 confirms maximum.

questions.question: A cylindrical can with volume V = πr²h must minimize surface area S = 2πr² + 2πrh. The optimal ratio h/r is:
questions.explanation: Substitute h = V/(πr²) into S, minimize with respect to r. Setting dS/dr = 0 gives r³ = V/(2π), which implies h = 2r at the optimum.
